zh.lifeSummary: Grimbald de ELLEL於1162年之前與Mrs Grimbald de ELLEL在英國結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Grimbald de ELLEL于1162年之前与Mrs Grimbald de ELLEL在英国结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: ELLEL
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: from a pet form of   Albrecht  .

variants.name: Albrecht
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Hans, Manfred, Otto, Horst, Klaus, Arno, Heinz, Milbert, Fritz, Gerhard.

German: from the personal name Albrecht, composed of the ancient Germanic elements adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. Compare Albert . This surname is also found elsewhere in central Europe, e.g. in Czechia.
Germanized or Americanized form of Slovenian Albreht: from the personal name Albreht, of ancient Germanic origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Grimble
meanings: English (Norfolk and Suffolk): variant of   Gribble   2.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: Grimbald de ELLEL married Mrs Grimbald de ELLEL before 1162, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Grimbald d’ELLEL a épousé Mrs Grimbald d’ELLEL avant 1162, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Grimbald de ELLEL hat vor 1162 in England Mrs Grimbald de ELLEL geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Grimbald de ELLEL lui sposato Mrs Grimbald de ELLEL prima del 1162, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Grimbald de ELLELは1162年以前にイギリスでMrs Grimbald de ELLELと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Grimbald de ELLEL(은)는 1162년 이전 영국에서 Mrs Grimbald de ELLEL(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Grimbald de ELLEL casou-se com Mrs Grimbald de ELLEL antes 1162, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Grimbald de ELLEL женился на женщине по имени Mrs Grimbald de ELLEL перед 1162 г. в местности Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Grimbald de ELLEL se casó con Mrs Grimbald de ELLEL antes de 1162, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
